```
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   base_ttl /    в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ  в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ  (1 + ОұГ—deps)   в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в”Ӯ   Г— pos_weight  в”Ӯ    в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

в•”в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•—

```

---

## Section Details

### Header (~4.5" tall)
| Element | Content |
|---|---|
| Title | "Correctness-Aware Caching for Agentic LLM Tool Calls" |
| Authors | Archit Kumar В· Maaz Hussain В· Divya Pothavajhyula В· Rohan Tolani |
| Affiliation | University of Michigan, CSE 585 |

---

### Left Column (~9" wide)

**Motivation (~8")**  
Key points (bullet or short paragraph):
- LLM agents call tools (price APIs, news feeds, etc.) sequentially to answer queries
- Caching reduces latency and API costs вҖ” but stale data corrupts multi-step reasoning
- A stale root-node result can route the agent down the wrong branch entirely, making all downstream calls useless
- Existing caching metrics (hit rate, staleness age) fail to capture decision correctness

**System Architecture (~6")**  
Three-box Canva diagram: `LangGraph Agent` вҶ’ `Cache Gateway (policy plug-in)` вҶ’ `API Simulator`

**Workflow 1: Investment Decision DAG (~7")**  
Create in Canva with rounded-rect nodes + arrows:
```
       [get_price(ticker)]   вҶҗ 3 downstream deps вҶ’ tighter TTL
              вҶ“
    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җ branch on price в”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    вҶ“                       вҶ“
[get_news_sentiment]    [get_trend]    вҶҗ 1 dep each вҶ’ relaxed TTL
    вҶ“                       вҶ“
 [SELL/HOLD]         [BUY/SELL/HOLD]
```

**Workflow 2: Portfolio Rebalancing DAG (~7")**  
Create in Canva:
```
[get_price(AAPL)] в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [compute_risk] в”Җв”Җв”җ
        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [compute_tax]  в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [decide]  (AAPL: 3 deps)
[get_price(GOOG)] в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [compute_risk] в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [decide] (GOOG: 2 deps)
[get_price(NVDA)] в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [compute_risk] в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [decide] (NVDA: 2 deps)
```

**Policy Formula (~3")**  
Styled formula box:
```
TTL = base_ttl / (1 + Оұ Г— downstream_deps) Г— position_weight
```

---

### Center Column (~11" wide) вҖ” Hero

**Figure 1 вҖ” Hero: Mismatch Rate Comparison** (`hero_mismatch.png`, ~16")  
Workflow-aware TTL cuts decision errors by **2.25Г—** on Investment Decision (2.7% вҶ’ 1.2%) and **2.0Г—** on Portfolio Rebalancing (6.5% вҶ’ 3.2%). The improvement is consistent across both workflows despite their fundamentally different topologies (branching fan-out vs. parallel fan-in), suggesting the insight generalizes.

**Figure 2 вҖ” Latency-Correctness Tradeoff** (`latency_tradeoff.png`, ~9")  
Workflow-aware is only ~25вҖ“39% slower than fixed TTL (110ms vs. 79вҖ“89ms) while achieving ~2Г— fewer decision errors. It dominates fixed TTL on correctness with modest latency cost, and is 2.5вҖ“3Г— faster than no-cache while sacrificing minimal correctness.

**Figure 3 вҖ” Hit Rate вү  Correctness** (`hit_rate_disconnect.png`, ~8")  
Portfolio fixed TTL achieves a 97.1% cache hit rate вҖ” yet still produces a 6.5% decision error rate. High hit rate does not imply correct decisions in multi-step agentic workflows. This challenges the assumption that optimizing for hit rate is a proxy for optimizing agent quality.

---

### Right Column (~9" wide)

**Key Numbers (~8")**  
Big-number callout blocks (styled in Canva):
- **2.25Г—** fewer decision errors (Investment Decision)
- **2.0Г—** fewer decision errors (Portfolio Rebalancing)
- **0%** wrong-branch errors with workflow-aware (vs. 27.3% of all mismatches with fixed TTL)

**Figure 4 вҖ” Branch Breakdown** (`branch_breakdown.png`, ~7")  
On the news_sentiment branch (routed through the high-fanout root node), workflow-aware reduces mismatches from 11.9% вҶ’ 0.5% вҖ” a **96% reduction** вҖ” by tightening the TTL at the stale price node. On the trend branch (same root, different threshold), the reduction is a smaller **19%** (1.6% вҶ’ 1.3%) since the price must move more to cause a mis-route. Wrong-branch errors are eliminated entirely (0% vs. 27.3% of all fixed-TTL mismatches).

**Results Tables (~8")**

Investment Decision:
| Policy | Hit Rate | Mismatch Rate | Avg Latency |
|---|---|---|---|
| No Cache | 0% | 0.0% | 280ms |
| Fixed TTL | 79.9% | 2.7% | 79ms |
| Workflow-Aware | 49.6% | 1.2% | 110ms |

Portfolio Rebalancing:
| Policy | Hit Rate | Mismatch Rate | Avg Latency |
|---|---|---|---|
| No Cache | 0% | 0.3% | 337ms |
| Fixed TTL | 97.1% | 6.5% | 89ms |
| Workflow-Aware | 91.6% | 3.2% | 111ms |

**Conclusion (~5")**
- Staleness impact is non-uniform вҖ” DAG position matters more than change rate alone
- Workflow-aware TTL reduces decision errors by ~2Г— with only ~40% latency overhead
- High hit rate is a poor proxy for correctness in agentic workflows

**Future Work (~4")**
- Real LLM integration (currently uses deterministic decision rules)
- Adaptive TTL learning (currently hand-tuned)
- Multi-agent cache sharing analysis
